era_context: Late Hellenistic period. Han Dynasty under Emperor Wu is at its height, conducting military campaigns and establishing the Silk Road. The Seleucid Empire is fragmenting. Rome is in the late Republic, approaching the Social War. In India, the Sunga Dynasty controls the Gangetic plain. In the Mediterranean, Hellenistic kingdoms are declining in the face of Roman expansion.

events.title: Han Dynasty diplomatic and military activities
events.description: Emperor Wu of Han continued his policy of military expansion and diplomatic engagement with neighboring states. Han forces were active in multiple theaters: against the Xiongnu in the north, in Central Asia securing trade routes, and in the south following the conquest of Nanyue. These activities were part of a comprehensive strategy to expand Han power and secure resources.

sources.name: Shiji (Records of the Grand Historian) by Sima Qian
Han Shu (Book of Han) by Ban Gu

events.certainty_note: Han military activities during this period are well-attested in general, but specific events in -113 are not clearly dated in surviving sources.

disconfirming_evidence: No specific events of -113 are clearly attested in surviving sources. The year is assigned an event by convention based on broader historical patterns. The Han military activities described are real historical processes, but they are not dated to -113 specifically.
historiographic_note: The year -113 has minimal specific documentation. Information is inferred from broader historical patterns and later compilations. Chinese sources provide the most detailed information, but they were compiled decades or centuries after the events. The year -113 itself has no special significance in surviving records and is included by convention as part of a continuous chronicle.

graph_edges.note: Han military expansion was ongoing during this period
